2606.04177.title: A Systematic Analysis of Linguistic Features in AI-Generated Text Detection Across Domains and Models
2606.04177.paper_summary: Interpretable linguistic features offer a promising approach for explaining why a given text appears machine-generated, particularly for non-expert users. However, existing findings on which features reliably indicate LLM-generated text remain fragmented across feature sets, models, and text domains. To address this gap, we conduct a large-scale empirical study assessing the robustness of linguistic signals for characterizing AI-generated text. Our analysis covers 284 interpretable linguistic features across outputs from 27 LLMs and ten text domains under cross-model and cross-domain generalization settings. We show that classifiers based solely on linguistic features can reliably distinguish AI-generated from human-written text. However, many previously proposed indicators prove strongly context-dependent, with the exception of measures of lexical richness, which remain robust signals across model families and text domains. These results demonstrate which linguistic signals generalize across contexts and provide a foundation for more reliable, interpretable analyses of AI-generated language.
2606.04177.paper_summary_zh: 可解釋的語言特徵為解釋為何特定文本看起來像是機器生成的提供了一種有前景的方法，特別是對於非專業用戶而言。<lb/>然而，現有的研究結果顯示，哪些特徵可靠地指示 LLM 生成的文本在特徵集、模型和文本領域之間仍然是零散的。<lb/>為了解決這一空白，我們進行了一項大規模的實證研究，評估語言信號在表徵 AI 生成文本方面的穩健性。<lb/>我們的分析涵蓋了 284 個可解釋的語言特徵，這些特徵來自 27 個 LLM 和十個文本領域，並在跨模型和跨領域的泛化設置下進行評估。<lb/>我們顯示，僅基於語言特徵的分類器可以可靠地區分 AI 生成的文本和人類撰寫的文本。<lb/>然而，許多先前提出的指標證明強烈依賴於上下文，除了詞彙豐富度的測量，這些在模型系列和文本領域之間仍然是穩健的信號。<lb/>這些結果展示了哪些語言信號在不同上下文中具有泛化能力，並為更可靠、可解釋的 AI 生成語言分析提供了基礎。
2606.04177.author: Yassir El Attar et.al.
2606.04177.authors: Yassir El Attar, Esra Dönmez, Maximilian Maurer, Agnieszka Falenska
